result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add-file <files>      Add protobuf file reference(s) to the gRPC project.
  add-url <url>         Add a protobuf url reference to the gRPC project.
  refresh <references>  Check remote protobuf references(s) for updates and replace them if a newer version is available. If no file or url is provided, all remote protobuf files will be updated.
  remove <references>   Remove protobuf references(s) from the gRPC project.
  list                  List the protobuf references of the gRPC project.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add-file <files>      Add protobuf file reference(s) to the gRPC project.
  add-url <url>         Add a protobuf url reference to the gRPC project.
  refresh <references>  Check remote protobuf references(s) for updates and replace them if a newer version is available. If no file or url is provided, all remote protobuf files will be updated.
  remove <references>   Remove protobuf references(s) from the gRPC project.
  list                  List the protobuf references of the gRPC project.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Add protobuf file reference(s) to the gRPC project.

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc add-file <files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <files>  The protobuf file reference(s). These can be a path to glob for local protobuf file(s).

Options:
  -p, --project <project>                                The path to the project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
  -s, --services <Both|Client|Default|None|Server>       The type of gRPC services that should be generated. If Default is specified, Both will be used for Web projects and Client will be used for non-Web projects.
  --access <Internal|Public>                             The access modifier to use for the generated C# classes. Default value is Public.
  -i, --additional-import-dirs <additional-import-dirs>  Additional directories to be used when resolving imports for the protobuf files. This is a semicolon separated list of paths.
  -?, -h, --help                                         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Add protobuf file reference(s) to the gRPC project.

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc add-file <files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <files>  The protobuf file reference(s). These can be a path to glob for local protobuf file(s).

Options:
  -p, --project <project>                                The path to the project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
  -s, --services <Both|Client|Default|None|Server>       The type of gRPC services that should be generated. If Default is specified, Both will be used for Web projects and Client will be used for non-Web projects.
  --access <Internal|Public>                             The access modifier to use for the generated C# classes. Default value is Public.
  -i, --additional-import-dirs <additional-import-dirs>  Additional directories to be used when resolving imports for the protobuf files. This is a semicolon separated list of paths.
  -?, -h, --help                                         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Add a protobuf url reference to the gRPC project.

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc add-url <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  The URL to a remote protobuf file.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>                                  Specify the download path for the remote protobuf file. This is a required option.
  -p, --project <project>                                The path to the project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
  -s, --services <Both|Client|Default|None|Server>       The type of gRPC services that should be generated. If Default is specified, Both will be used for Web projects and Client will be used for non-Web projects.
  -i, --additional-import-dirs <additional-import-dirs>  Additional directories to be used when resolving imports for the protobuf files. This is a semicolon separated list of paths.
  --access <Internal|Public>                             The access modifier to use for the generated C# classes. Default value is Public.
  -?, -h, --help                                         Show help and usage information

  -s, --services <Both|Client|Default|None|Server>       The type of gRPC services that should be generated. If Default is specified, Both will be used for Web projects and Client will be used for non-Web projects.
  -i, --additional-import-dirs <additional-import-dirs>  Additional directories to be used when resolving imports for the protobuf files. This is a semicolon separated list of paths.
  --access <Internal|Public>                             The access modifier to use for the generated C# classes. Default value is Public.
  -?, -h, --help                                         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  List the protobuf references of the gRPC project.

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc list [options]

Options:
  -p, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Check remote protobuf references(s) for updates and replace them if a newer version is available. If no file or url is provided, all remote protobuf files will be updated.

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc refresh [<references>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <references>  The URL(s) or file path(s) to remote protobuf references(s) that should be updated. Leave this argument empty to refresh all remote references.

Options:
  -p, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
  --dry-run                Output a list of file(s) that will be updated without downloading any new content.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Check remote protobuf references(s) for updates and replace them if a newer version is available. If no file or url is provided, all remote protobuf files will be updated.

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc refresh [<references>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <references>  The URL(s) or file path(s) to remote protobuf references(s) that should be updated. Leave this argument empty to refresh all remote references.

Options:
  -p, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
  --dry-run                Output a list of file(s) that will be updated without downloading any new content.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Remove protobuf references(s) from the gRPC project.

Usage:
  dotnet-grpc remove <references>... [options]

Arguments:
  <references>  The URL(s) or file path(s) of the protobuf references to remove.

Options:
  -p, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information
